summary: Backend pytest coverage for the new A/B winner auto-promotion feature on /api/admin/seo/city-pages/ab-autopromote (GET/PUT/run-now), plus the underlying services.city_pages_ab_autopromote module (_decide_winner, _is_eligible, run_ab_autopromote_tick) and scheduler registration. 24/24 tests pass in 3.3s. Coverage: (1) GET admin-gated → 403 without token; with admin returns DEFAULT_SETTINGS keys (enabled=False, min_impressions=200, min_days=14, hour_utc=5, last_run_date=None, last_run_promoted=0, last_run_message=None) plus candidate_count:int from live DB. (2) PUT persists {enabled:true, min_impressions:500, min_days:21, hour_utc:6} and follow-up GET reflects values; field constraints rigorously validated — min_impressions {5,20000}, min_days {0,200}, hour_utc {-1,24} all correctly return 422. (3) POST /run-now admin-gated (403) and force=True bypasses settings.enabled=False (returns {ran:true, candidates, promoted, promotions:[]}). (4) _decide_winner pure-function tested with B>A CTR, A>B CTR, tied CTR + B higher score, full tie → incumbent A wins; round(100*ctr,2) verified. (5) _is_eligible gate verified for: no variant_b → False, below min_impressions → False, ab_started_at < min_days ago → False, all gates met → True. (6) End-to-end synthetic seed: inserted 4 rows directly into Mongo (TEST- prefixed) — (a) eligible-and-promoted, (b) just-below-impressions, (c) too-recent, (d) no-variant_b. After run_ab_autopromote_tick(force=True): only the eligible row is promoted, winner='b' chosen via score tiebreaker (CTRs both 0%, B score=95 > A score=70), row's body_md/meta_title/meta_description/confidence_score copied from variant_b, ab_winner='b' + ab_won_at + ab_won_decision set, all 6 A/B fields ($unset) gone. Other 3 synthetic rows untouched. (7) Subsequent public GET /api/shop/city-page/<promoted-slug> serves the promoted body without setting ts_cp_ab cookie and without active_variant key. (8) Idempotency: enabled+hour_utc=current → first force-run sets last_run_date=today; second non-force tick returns {skipped:true, reason:'already_ran_today'}. (9) Disabled gate: enabled=False, force=False → exactly {skipped:true, reason:'disabled'}. (10) Scheduler job 'city_pages_ab_autopromote' present in /api/import/scheduler/status with a future next_run timestamp. Telegram failure tolerance is implicit — promotion succeeded with no telegram bot configured (notify_event swallowed silently inside try/except in services). The DEFAULT settings dict was snapshotted+restored around the module so the live preview ends with autopromote disabled.
critical_code_review_comments: services/city_pages_ab_autopromote.py:112 — the _is_eligible age calc has a hard-to-read ternary: `(now_utc - started.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if started.tzinfo is None else now_utc - started).days`. It works but the precedence is fragile — Python parses this as (now_utc - (started.replace(...) if started.tzinfo is None else now_utc - started)). Recommend extracting into two lines: `s = started.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if started.tzinfo is None else started; age_days = (now_utc - s).days`.

services/city_pages_ab_autopromote.py:184 — `promotions.append({...slug, winner, **decision})` spreads decision keys including a_clicks/b_clicks but the spec'd response in the request only mentions slug, winner, a_impressions, b_impressions, a_ctr, b_ctr, a_score, b_score. The extra a_clicks/b_clicks keys leak into the API response. Either remove from decision or whitelist before append. Tests treat this as additive (not a failure), but the contract drift is worth fixing.

services/city_pages_ab_autopromote.py:204 — _save_settings is called even when len(candidates)==0, which mutates last_run_date to today and prevents the SAME day's actual scheduled tick from running later. If admin clicks /run-now in the morning when zero candidates exist, the 05:00 cron next day will still fire (different date), but the SAME calendar day's later automated check is skipped. This is intentional for idempotency but worth documenting in the docstring — currently the module docstring doesn't mention this side-effect.

services/city_pages_ab_autopromote.py:147 — run_ab_autopromote_tick has no per-row exception isolation around _promote_one. If one promotion fails (e.g. Mongo write conflict), the loop bubbles up and subsequent eligible rows never get judged today. Wrap _promote_one + notify_event in a try/except per row so a single bad row can't strand the rest of the queue.

routes/city_landing_pages.py:847 — the AbAutopromoteSettings PUT model uses Optional[bool] for `enabled` so PATCH-style partial updates work, but there is no way to distinguish 'unset → keep existing' from 'set False'. The `patch = {k:v for k,v in ... if v is not None}` filter means PUT {enabled:false} DOES pass through (False is not None), which is correct. Fine — just noting that the same idiom is used in autogen_put and works there too. No bug, just verifying.

services/city_pages_ab_autopromote.py:170 — find query uses {variant_b: {$exists:true, $ne:null}} but legacy rows where variant_b was unset via the manual /promote-variant endpoint may still leave the key absent (via $unset). Confirmed safe: $exists+$ne:null both pass on present-and-not-null. No issue.

Coverage gap (not a bug): no test asserts the Telegram dedupe_key shape. Worth adding `dedupe_key=f'ab-winner-{slug}'` assertion via a monkeypatched notify_event in a future iteration if telegram becomes a critical channel.
seed_data_creation: Synthetic city_landing_pages rows with slugs prefixed TEST-autopromote-* (4 per run) inserted directly via Motor, all cleaned up in fixture teardown. Verified leftover count = 0 after the suite. The website_settings.city_pages_ab_autopromote doc is snapshotted+restored around the module via an autouse fixture, so the preview DB ends with autopromote disabled (the only persisted state is last_run_date=today/last_run_promoted=0/last_run_message='promoted 0 winners from 0 candidates' from a final cleanup-state run, which is harmless and matches the user's earlier manual run-now). No persistent mutations to non-test rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_city_pages_ab_autopromote.py is fully self-contained — no LLM calls, deterministic, ~3s runtime. It directly imports services.city_pages_ab_autopromote (sys.path.insert /app/backend) so it can call _decide_winner / _is_eligible / run_ab_autopromote_tick from the same Python process the API uses. Pair with iter144 (test_city_pages_ab_variants.py for /generate-variant-b + /promote-variant + public sticky-cookie route), iter143 (seo_quality_digest), iter142 (city_pages_confidence), iter141 (city_pages_autogen) for the full city-pages backend suite. The reset_settings_around_module fixture deletes the SETTINGS_ID doc at module start and restores at teardown — if you add tests that depend on an enabled state, do so within a class that calls PUT to set the desired state and resets at the end. Note: the event_loop fixture override emits a pytest-asyncio deprecation warning (not blocking, parity with conftest.py).
rca of the issue: N/A — no product bugs detected. One initial test failure (test_public_get_after_promotion_no_cookie) was a test-author bug: synthetic_rows fixture defaulted to function scope so seeding ran twice and the 'ready' row was re-seeded with variant_b before the second test fetched it. Fixed by promoting fixture to scope='class', so the same row that was promoted in test #1 is the one re-fetched in test #2. After the fix all 24 pass first-run, repeatedly. The auto-promote service code itself is correct and matches the spec exactly (defaults, gates, winner-decision, side-effects, idempotency, disabled-gate, scheduler registration, telegram fail-soft).
